description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a series of rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a linear, slightly staggered configuration. The panels are mounted on a robust, low-profile base structure, likely made of metal, which provides stability and support. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, with a dark coloration typical of monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon solar cells. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and possibly composite materials, with visible structural reinforcements and a few colored accent panels, possibly for identification or safety. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling on the exterior, suggesting these components may be housed internally or beneath the panels. The overall design is compact and modular, suitable for placement on flat urban surfaces such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A compact, modular solar panel array with a dark, reflective surface mounted on a sturdy metal base, designed for urban energy generation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the staggered arrangement of the panels on a low-profile base.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the narrow profile of the installation and the visible layering of the panels and support structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the linear alignment of the panels and the colored accent panels on the base.
description_view_3: Reverse end view showing the depth of the base and the arrangement of the panels from the opposite perspective.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metal (likely aluminum) frame with a reinforced base
attribute_array_configuration: linear row of approximately 6 panels, slightly staggered
